title: Terminal int8 tanh block
hypothesis: A bounded int8 tanh activation block can be implemented and physically characterized well enough to serve as the next lower-layer prerequisite for broadening terminal nonlinear activation evaluation beyond sigmoid.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can one bounded int8 tanh block produce accepted physical implementation metrics suitable for later integrated and Layer 2 evaluation use?
include: one bounded int8 tanh block candidate
one bounded physical sweep on the target platform
exclude: integrated nm1 architecture-block evaluation in the first pass
native fp16 tanh in the first pass
follow_on_broad_sweep: only if the first tanh block is accepted and further circuit-family exploration becomes necessary

expected_benefit: provides real physical timing, power, and area for a second bounded int8 nonlinear activation block
unblocks a technically credible integrated nm1 tanh-enable follow-on and later bounded Layer 2 tanh direct-output work
risks: the bounded int8 tanh may still be too costly or too inaccurate in the first chosen implementation style
the first pass may need symmetry or saturation-oriented wrapper adjustments before it is consumable by an integrated nm1 follow-on
later fp16-end-to-end use may still require an explicit conversion strategy or a separate native-fp16 follow-on block

expected_result.reason: the first goal is simply to obtain accepted physical implementation metrics for one bounded int8 tanh block
